list.title: Flow-DPPO: Divergence Proximal Policy Optimization for Flow Matching Models

extra.desc: Flow-DPPO replaces ratio clipping with divergence proximal constraints in flow matching models, improving training stability and multi-objective optimization through exact KL divergence computation.

list.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories

extra.desc: A multi-agent framework automates data journalism by generating evidence-grounded, multimodal news stories while maintaining transparency and verifiability.

list.title: WorldOlympiad: Can Your World Model Survive a Triathlon?

extra.desc: WorldOlympiad presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating video-based world models across physical faithfulness, geometric consistency, and interaction fidelity, revealing significant gaps in current generative models' capabilities.

list.title: Attention Amnesia in Hybrid LLMs: When CoT Fine-Tuning Breaks Long-Range Recall, and How to Fix It

extra.desc: Chain-of-thought supervised fine-tuning degrades long-context recall in hybrid linear-attention models by biasing attention gradients toward short-range patterns, but a training-free method called QK-Restore can restore long-context capabilities by reverting query-key projections while preserving reasoning performance.

list.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL

extra.desc: DRPO improves LLM reinforcement learning stability by replacing hard masks with smooth regularization that provides continuous gradient corrections beyond trust-region boundaries.

list.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents

extra.desc: EEVEE is a novel test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents that handles heterogeneous data streams through task clustering and co-evolving router-prompt optimization.

list.title: SearchSwarm: Towards Delegation Intelligence in Agentic LLMs for Long-Horizon Deep Research

extra.desc: A large language model trained on synthesized delegation intelligence achieves superior performance on long-horizon research tasks through task decomposition and subagent coordination.

list.title: One Token per Multimodal Evidence: Latent Memory for Resource-Constrained QA

extra.desc: Latent Memory introduces a compressed representation approach for external memory in question answering, reducing token consumption and storage requirements while maintaining competitive performance across text-only and multimodal benchmarks.

list.title: Struct-Searcher: Agentic Structural Thinking Advances Multimodal Deep Information Seeking

extra.desc: Struct-Searcher introduces a belief revision theory-based structural agentic workflow for multimodal information seeking that improves accuracy over existing vision-language models and deep research agents.

list.title: How Does Reasoning Flow? Tracing Attention-Induced Information Flow for Targeted RL in LLMs

extra.desc: FlowTracer is an RL framework that uses attention-induced graphs to trace reasoning flows and assign token-level credit based on global information propagation structures.

list.title: When the Chain of Thought Knows Better: Failure Modes in Multi-Turn Reasoning Models

extra.desc: Multi-turn reasoning models exhibit hidden alignment failures that are masked by traditional evaluation methods, revealing vulnerabilities through a trace-level diagnostic framework that identifies distinct failure modes including context-injection failures.

list.title: Emergent Misalignment Can Be Induced by Sycophancy and Reversed via Alignment Gating

extra.desc: Sycophancy fine-tuning contributes to emergent misalignment in language models, which can be reversed using Alignment Gating—a method that inserts learnable gates to identify and control unsafe responses while maintaining general capabilities.

list.title: ARM: An AutoRegressive Large Multimodal Model with Unified Discrete Representations

extra.desc: ARM demonstrates a unified autoregressive framework for image understanding, generation, and editing through discrete semantic tokenization and reinforcement learning optimization.

list.title: Bridging the Agent-World Gap: Text World Models for LLM-based Agents

extra.desc: Text world models serve as transition models for LLM-based agents in interactive environments, enabling planning and efficient learning by predicting environmental changes from textual states and actions.

list.title: Dynamic Linear Attention

extra.desc: DLA addresses limitations in long-context LLMs by introducing adaptive state merging and capacity-bounded memory modeling for improved multi-state linear attention.

list.title: SCAIL-2: Unifying Controlled Character Animation with End-to-end In-Context Conditioning

extra.desc: SCAIL-2 enables end-to-end character animation by directly transferring motion from driving videos without intermediate representations, using unified task decomposition and synthetic data generation.

list.title: Workflow-GYM: Towards Long-Horizon Evaluation of Computer-use Agentic tasks in Real-World Professional Fields

extra.desc: Current AI agents struggle with long-horizon professional GUI workflows, achieving low success rates due to issues with workflow consistency and domain-specific software understanding.

list.title: Test-Time Gradient Guidance of Flow Policies in Reinforcement Learning

extra.desc: QGF is an RL algorithm that improves policies at test time by using a value gradient to guide a pre-trained flow policy, avoiding training-time instability while maintaining competitive performance.

list.title: MilliVid: Hierarchical Latents for Long-Range Consistency in Video Generation

extra.desc: Video generative models achieve improved long-range consistency through coarse-to-fine token generation using a multi-scale autoencoder and diffusion model architecture.

list.title: ABot-Earth 0.5: Generative 3D Earth Model

extra.desc: ABot-Earth 0.5 generates realistic 3D environments from satellite imagery using 3D Gaussian Splatting representation, enabling fast synthesis and real-time visualization for Embodied AI applications.

list.title: Online Skill Learning for Web Agents via State-Grounded Dynamic Retrieval

extra.desc: State-Grounded Dynamic Retrieval enables web agents to dynamically reuse skills based on current webpage state rather than fixed task-level strategies, improving automation performance across multiple domains.

list.title: BenSyc: Benchmarking Conversational Sycophancy and Human Alignment in LLMs for Bengali Contexts

extra.desc: Researchers create BenSyc, a benchmark for evaluating conversational sycophancy in Bengali contexts, revealing challenges in distinguishing empathetic support from validation and escalation in emotionally sensitive dialogues.

list.title: What Should Agents Say? Action-state Communication for Efficient Multi-Agent Systems

extra.desc: Multi-agent systems using large language models suffer from inefficient token consumption in agent-to-agent communication, which PACT addresses by structuring messages as compact action-state records that improve performance-cost trade-offs across different system architectures.

list.title: VoLo: A Physical Orchestrator for Open-Vocabulary Long-Horizon Manipulation

extra.desc: VoLoAgent enables physical orchestration by integrating vision-language models with robot capabilities for open-vocabulary long-horizon manipulation tasks.

list.title: Trust Functions: Near-Lossless Weak-to-Strong Generalization by Learning When to Trust the Weak Teacher

extra.desc: Trust functions enable effective weak-to-strong generalization by identifying reliable weak labels for training, achieving performance comparable to ground-truth supervision across multiple domains.

list.title: Send a SCOUT First: Pre-hoc Reasoning for Adaptive Detector Allocation in Prompt-Injection Defense

extra.desc: SCOUT framework dynamically allocates prompt-injection detection by predicting detector reliability and latency, improving safety and efficiency over fixed single-detector approaches.

list.title: Precision Is Not Faithfulness: Coverage-Aware Evaluation of Grounded Generation with a Complete Oracle

extra.desc: Reference-free faithfulness metrics suffer from a blind spot measuring only precision, leading to rewards for abstention; completeness in deterministic domains enables measurement of both precision and recall, revealing that high-precision models often have poor fact coverage.

list.title: AsyncWebRL: Efficient Multi-Step RL for Visual Web Agents

extra.desc: AsyncWebRL improves vision-language web agent training through asynchronous reinforcement learning and trajectory normalization modifications, achieving faster throughput and better performance on challenging tasks.

list.title: SDR: Set-Distance Rewards for Radiology Report Generation

extra.desc: Set-based rewards using embedding distances improve chest X-ray report generation by enabling effective post-training and test-time selection without requiring causal reasoning structures.

list.title: Agents' Last Exam

extra.desc: Agents' Last Exam (ALE) is a benchmark for evaluating AI agents on long-term, economically valuable real-world tasks across 13 industry clusters with 1K+ tasks, revealing significant gaps between benchmark performance and practical deployment.

list.title: Robotic Policy Adaptation via Weight-Space Meta-Learning

extra.desc: WIZARD is a weight-space meta-learning framework that generates task-specific LoRA parameters for frozen VLA policies using language instructions and demonstration videos, enabling efficient task adaptation without fine-tuning.

list.title: Light-WAM: Efficient World Action Models with State-Fusion Action Decoding

extra.desc: Light-WAM is a lightweight world action model for robot manipulation that uses a compact video backbone and downsampled latent space for efficient future-video supervision, combined with a StateFusionActionExpert for direct action prediction.

list.title: DEI: Diversity in Evolutionary Inference for Quality-Diversity Search

extra.desc: A distributed Quality-Diversity search framework uses heterogeneous large language models as mutation operators to enhance evolutionary inference, demonstrating that model diversity improves performance over homogeneous parallel approaches.

list.title: SigmaScale: LLM Compression with SVD-based Low-Rank Decomposition and Learned Scaling Matrices

extra.desc: SigmaScale learns auxiliary scaling matrices to improve truncated SVD-based LLM compression by adapting to individual weight structures through activation-aware transformations.

list.title: Pruning and Distilling Mixture-of-Experts into Dense Language Models

extra.desc: A systematic framework converts mixture-of-experts models into dense architectures through expert scoring, selection, grouping, and knowledge distillation, achieving superior performance and efficiency compared to traditional pruning methods.

list.title: Where Rectified Flows Leak: Characterising Membership Signals Along the Interpolation Path

extra.desc: Rectified Flows retain subtle training data traces that accumulate during training and can be exploited for membership inference attacks.

list.title: EmpiriGraph-Psy: A Dataset and LLM Pipeline for Extracting Empirical Relation Graphs from Psychology Abstracts

extra.desc: Variable-centered empirical graph extraction maps psychology abstracts to typed graphs with normalized variables and empirical relations, achieving improved performance through staged pipeline approaches.

list.title: Phase Marginalization for Patch-Grid Instability in Vision Transformers

extra.desc: Phase Marginalization is a post-hoc method that addresses phase-dependent instability in Vision Transformers by evaluating structured patch-grid phases and aggregating outputs in the original image coordinate system.

list.title: Skill-3D: Evolving Scene-Aware Skills for Agentic 3D Spatial Reasoning

extra.desc: Skill-3D framework enables agents to learn scene-aware skills through self-evolving memory and skill libraries, improving tool utilization in 3D spatial reasoning tasks.

list.title: Liberating LLM Capabilities in Full-Duplex Speech Models

extra.desc: A text-first tri-channel speech interface enables real-time interaction with visible text output alongside spoken responses, demonstrating superior performance in full-duplex conversational tasks.

list.title: WorldCraft: From Camera Navigation to Object Manipulation in Interactive Video World Models

extra.desc: WorldCraft extends interactive video world models to enable object-level trajectory control while maintaining camera navigation capabilities through specialized control pipelines.

list.title: A Geometric Account of Activation Steering through Angle-Norm Decomposition

extra.desc: Research challenges the assumption that hidden-state norms carry concept-relevant information in language models, demonstrating that concepts are primarily represented in angular structure while norm remains crucial for steering stability and effectiveness across multiple models.

list.title: Reasoning over Grammar: Can Synthetic Linguistic Reasoning Traces Enhance Low-Resource Machine Translation?

extra.desc: Large language models can improve translation for low-resource languages through structured linguistic reasoning traces, with the most significant benefits occurring during inference rather than training.

list.title: Whisper Hallucination Detection and Mitigation via Hidden Representation Steering and Sparse AutoEncoders

extra.desc: Research demonstrates that hallucinations in Whisper ASR can be detected and reduced using internal representations from audio encoder activations and Sparse AutoEncoder latents, achieving significant hallucination rate reduction with minimal speech transcription degradation.

list.title: CIPER: A Unified Framework for Cross-view Image-retrieval and Pose-estimation

extra.desc: CIPER is a unified cross-view geo-localization framework that simultaneously performs city-scale retrieval and precise 3-DoF pose estimation using a shared transformer encoder and two-way pose decoder.

list.title: OmniCap-IF: Benchmarking and Improving Instruction Following Abilities for Omni-Video Captioning

extra.desc: OmniCap-IF is introduced as the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating instruction-following capabilities in omni-modal captioning, revealing significant performance disparities and a format-content tradeoff in multi-modal reasoning.

list.title: SlimSearcher: Training Efficiency-Aware Web Agents via Adaptive Reward Gating

extra.desc: SlimSearcher is a framework that improves efficiency in deep research agents by combining Pareto-efficient trajectory filtering and adaptive reward shaping to reduce computational costs while maintaining accuracy.

list.title: Hardening Agent Benchmarks with Adversarial Hacker-Fixer Loops

extra.desc: Researchers identify widespread vulnerabilities in agent benchmark verification systems and develop an automated iterative process using LLM agents to create robust verifiers that resist exploitation while maintaining legitimate task performance.

list.title: LatentSkill: From In-Context Textual Skills to In-Weight Latent Skills for LLM Agents

extra.desc: LatentSkill enables efficient deployment of textual skills in agent systems by converting them into LoRA adapters stored in weight space, reducing context overhead while maintaining modularity and composability.

list.title: Interpreting and Steering a Text-to-Speech Language Model with Sparse Autoencoders

extra.desc: Sparse autoencoders trained on language model representations reveal interpretable features for speech synthesis that can be manipulated to control linguistic and prosodic attributes.

list.title: Kwai Keye-VL-2.0 Technical Report

extra.desc: Kwai Keye-VL-2.0-30B-A3B is an open-source Mixture-of-Experts multimodal foundation model that enables long-video understanding and agentic intelligence through DeepSeek Sparse Attention and specialized training infrastructure.

list.title: IR3DE: A Linear Router for Large Language Models

extra.desc: A ridge regression-based routing method achieves competitive performance in selecting domain-expert LLMs for different tasks while enabling dynamic addition/removal of experts without retraining.

list.title: PsychoSafe: Eliciting Psychologically-Informed Refusals in Large Language Models

extra.desc: A psychologically-informed refusal framework called PsychoSafe is developed for large language models to improve harmful request handling through structured supportive communication, showing enhanced refusal quality and resource referral while maintaining performance on non-refusal tasks.

list.title: BrainSurgery: Reproducible and Reliable Declarative Weight Manipulations for Model Editing and Upcycling

extra.desc: BrainSurgery is a tool for robust and reproducible tensor manipulation of neural network checkpoints through declarative YAML plans with built-in validation.

list.title: UniPET: a universal network for high-quality PET image denoising across varied dose reduction factors

extra.desc: A universal PET image denoising framework addresses variability in dose reduction factors through domain generalization techniques and region-aware learning strategies.

list.title: U-TTT: Towards Generalizable PET Image Denoising via Test-Time Training

extra.desc: A novel U-shaped deep learning model with test-time training layers and dual-domain adaptation mechanisms achieves robust PET image denoising under distribution shifts.

list.title: Role-Agent: Bootstrapping LLM Agents via Dual-Role Evolution

extra.desc: Role-Agent framework enables LLM agents to function as both agent and environment through bootstrapped co-evolution, improving performance via environment-aware reasoning and targeted practice.

list.title: Late-Layer Fusion is Enough: Dual-Path Vision Token Routing for Multimodal Large Language Models under Visual Saturation

extra.desc: Research reveals that vision and text tokens in multimodal models evolve asynchronously, leading to inefficient computation; a new asymmetric routing framework reduces visual processing overhead while maintaining performance.

list.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism

extra.desc: MemDreamer addresses long-video understanding challenges by decoupling perception and reasoning through hierarchical graph memory and agentic exploration, achieving state-of-the-art performance with reduced computational overhead.

list.title: Retrospective Harness Optimization: Improving LLM Agents via Self-Preference over Trajectory Rollouts

extra.desc: Retrospective Harness Optimization (RHO) is a self-supervised method that improves AI agent performance by optimizing agent harness using only past trajectories through diverse task selection, parallel re-solving, and self-validation techniques.

list.title: Lip Forcing: Few-Step Autoregressive Diffusion for Real-time Lip Synchronization

extra.desc: Autoregressive diffusion method for video-to-video lip synchronization achieves real-time performance through distillation and optimized inference schedules.

list.title: Flow-DPPO: Divergence Proximal Policy Optimization for Flow Matching Models

extra.desc: Flow-DPPO replaces ratio clipping with divergence proximal constraints in flow matching models, improving training stability and multi-objective optimization through exact KL divergence computation.

list.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories

extra.desc: A multi-agent framework automates data journalism by generating evidence-grounded, multimodal news stories while maintaining transparency and verifiability.

list.title: WorldOlympiad: Can Your World Model Survive a Triathlon?

extra.desc: WorldOlympiad presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating video-based world models across physical faithfulness, geometric consistency, and interaction fidelity, revealing significant gaps in current generative models' capabilities.

list.title: Attention Amnesia in Hybrid LLMs: When CoT Fine-Tuning Breaks Long-Range Recall, and How to Fix It

extra.desc: Chain-of-thought supervised fine-tuning degrades long-context recall in hybrid linear-attention models by biasing attention gradients toward short-range patterns, but a training-free method called QK-Restore can restore long-context capabilities by reverting query-key projections while preserving reasoning performance.

list.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL

extra.desc: DRPO improves LLM reinforcement learning stability by replacing hard masks with smooth regularization that provides continuous gradient corrections beyond trust-region boundaries.

list.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents

extra.desc: EEVEE is a novel test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents that handles heterogeneous data streams through task clustering and co-evolving router-prompt optimization.

list.title: SearchSwarm: Towards Delegation Intelligence in Agentic LLMs for Long-Horizon Deep Research

extra.desc: A large language model trained on synthesized delegation intelligence achieves superior performance on long-horizon research tasks through task decomposition and subagent coordination.

list.title: One Token per Multimodal Evidence: Latent Memory for Resource-Constrained QA

extra.desc: Latent Memory introduces a compressed representation approach for external memory in question answering, reducing token consumption and storage requirements while maintaining competitive performance across text-only and multimodal benchmarks.

list.title: Struct-Searcher: Agentic Structural Thinking Advances Multimodal Deep Information Seeking

extra.desc: Struct-Searcher introduces a belief revision theory-based structural agentic workflow for multimodal information seeking that improves accuracy over existing vision-language models and deep research agents.

list.title: How Does Reasoning Flow? Tracing Attention-Induced Information Flow for Targeted RL in LLMs

extra.desc: FlowTracer is an RL framework that uses attention-induced graphs to trace reasoning flows and assign token-level credit based on global information propagation structures.

list.title: When the Chain of Thought Knows Better: Failure Modes in Multi-Turn Reasoning Models

extra.desc: Multi-turn reasoning models exhibit hidden alignment failures that are masked by traditional evaluation methods, revealing vulnerabilities through a trace-level diagnostic framework that identifies distinct failure modes including context-injection failures.

list.title: Emergent Misalignment Can Be Induced by Sycophancy and Reversed via Alignment Gating

extra.desc: Sycophancy fine-tuning contributes to emergent misalignment in language models, which can be reversed using Alignment Gating—a method that inserts learnable gates to identify and control unsafe responses while maintaining general capabilities.

list.title: ARM: An AutoRegressive Large Multimodal Model with Unified Discrete Representations

extra.desc: ARM demonstrates a unified autoregressive framework for image understanding, generation, and editing through discrete semantic tokenization and reinforcement learning optimization.

list.title: Bridging the Agent-World Gap: Text World Models for LLM-based Agents

extra.desc: Text world models serve as transition models for LLM-based agents in interactive environments, enabling planning and efficient learning by predicting environmental changes from textual states and actions.

list.title: Dynamic Linear Attention

extra.desc: DLA addresses limitations in long-context LLMs by introducing adaptive state merging and capacity-bounded memory modeling for improved multi-state linear attention.

list.title: SCAIL-2: Unifying Controlled Character Animation with End-to-end In-Context Conditioning

extra.desc: SCAIL-2 enables end-to-end character animation by directly transferring motion from driving videos without intermediate representations, using unified task decomposition and synthetic data generation.

list.title: Workflow-GYM: Towards Long-Horizon Evaluation of Computer-use Agentic tasks in Real-World Professional Fields

extra.desc: Current AI agents struggle with long-horizon professional GUI workflows, achieving low success rates due to issues with workflow consistency and domain-specific software understanding.

list.title: Test-Time Gradient Guidance of Flow Policies in Reinforcement Learning

extra.desc: QGF is an RL algorithm that improves policies at test time by using a value gradient to guide a pre-trained flow policy, avoiding training-time instability while maintaining competitive performance.

list.title: MilliVid: Hierarchical Latents for Long-Range Consistency in Video Generation

extra.desc: Video generative models achieve improved long-range consistency through coarse-to-fine token generation using a multi-scale autoencoder and diffusion model architecture.

list.title: ABot-Earth 0.5: Generative 3D Earth Model

extra.desc: ABot-Earth 0.5 generates realistic 3D environments from satellite imagery using 3D Gaussian Splatting representation, enabling fast synthesis and real-time visualization for Embodied AI applications.

list.title: Online Skill Learning for Web Agents via State-Grounded Dynamic Retrieval

extra.desc: State-Grounded Dynamic Retrieval enables web agents to dynamically reuse skills based on current webpage state rather than fixed task-level strategies, improving automation performance across multiple domains.

list.title: BenSyc: Benchmarking Conversational Sycophancy and Human Alignment in LLMs for Bengali Contexts

extra.desc: Researchers create BenSyc, a benchmark for evaluating conversational sycophancy in Bengali contexts, revealing challenges in distinguishing empathetic support from validation and escalation in emotionally sensitive dialogues.

list.title: What Should Agents Say? Action-state Communication for Efficient Multi-Agent Systems

extra.desc: Multi-agent systems using large language models suffer from inefficient token consumption in agent-to-agent communication, which PACT addresses by structuring messages as compact action-state records that improve performance-cost trade-offs across different system architectures.

list.title: VoLo: A Physical Orchestrator for Open-Vocabulary Long-Horizon Manipulation

extra.desc: VoLoAgent enables physical orchestration by integrating vision-language models with robot capabilities for open-vocabulary long-horizon manipulation tasks.

list.title: Trust Functions: Near-Lossless Weak-to-Strong Generalization by Learning When to Trust the Weak Teacher

extra.desc: Trust functions enable effective weak-to-strong generalization by identifying reliable weak labels for training, achieving performance comparable to ground-truth supervision across multiple domains.

list.title: Send a SCOUT First: Pre-hoc Reasoning for Adaptive Detector Allocation in Prompt-Injection Defense

extra.desc: SCOUT framework dynamically allocates prompt-injection detection by predicting detector reliability and latency, improving safety and efficiency over fixed single-detector approaches.

list.title: Precision Is Not Faithfulness: Coverage-Aware Evaluation of Grounded Generation with a Complete Oracle

extra.desc: Reference-free faithfulness metrics suffer from a blind spot measuring only precision, leading to rewards for abstention; completeness in deterministic domains enables measurement of both precision and recall, revealing that high-precision models often have poor fact coverage.

list.title: AsyncWebRL: Efficient Multi-Step RL for Visual Web Agents

extra.desc: AsyncWebRL improves vision-language web agent training through asynchronous reinforcement learning and trajectory normalization modifications, achieving faster throughput and better performance on challenging tasks.

list.title: SDR: Set-Distance Rewards for Radiology Report Generation

extra.desc: Set-based rewards using embedding distances improve chest X-ray report generation by enabling effective post-training and test-time selection without requiring causal reasoning structures.

list.title: Agents' Last Exam

extra.desc: Agents' Last Exam (ALE) is a benchmark for evaluating AI agents on long-term, economically valuable real-world tasks across 13 industry clusters with 1K+ tasks, revealing significant gaps between benchmark performance and practical deployment.

list.title: Robotic Policy Adaptation via Weight-Space Meta-Learning

extra.desc: WIZARD is a weight-space meta-learning framework that generates task-specific LoRA parameters for frozen VLA policies using language instructions and demonstration videos, enabling efficient task adaptation without fine-tuning.

list.title: Light-WAM: Efficient World Action Models with State-Fusion Action Decoding

extra.desc: Light-WAM is a lightweight world action model for robot manipulation that uses a compact video backbone and downsampled latent space for efficient future-video supervision, combined with a StateFusionActionExpert for direct action prediction.

list.title: DEI: Diversity in Evolutionary Inference for Quality-Diversity Search

extra.desc: A distributed Quality-Diversity search framework uses heterogeneous large language models as mutation operators to enhance evolutionary inference, demonstrating that model diversity improves performance over homogeneous parallel approaches.

list.title: SigmaScale: LLM Compression with SVD-based Low-Rank Decomposition and Learned Scaling Matrices

extra.desc: SigmaScale learns auxiliary scaling matrices to improve truncated SVD-based LLM compression by adapting to individual weight structures through activation-aware transformations.

list.title: Pruning and Distilling Mixture-of-Experts into Dense Language Models

extra.desc: A systematic framework converts mixture-of-experts models into dense architectures through expert scoring, selection, grouping, and knowledge distillation, achieving superior performance and efficiency compared to traditional pruning methods.

list.title: Where Rectified Flows Leak: Characterising Membership Signals Along the Interpolation Path

extra.desc: Rectified Flows retain subtle training data traces that accumulate during training and can be exploited for membership inference attacks.

list.title: EmpiriGraph-Psy: A Dataset and LLM Pipeline for Extracting Empirical Relation Graphs from Psychology Abstracts

extra.desc: Variable-centered empirical graph extraction maps psychology abstracts to typed graphs with normalized variables and empirical relations, achieving improved performance through staged pipeline approaches.

list.title: Phase Marginalization for Patch-Grid Instability in Vision Transformers

extra.desc: Phase Marginalization is a post-hoc method that addresses phase-dependent instability in Vision Transformers by evaluating structured patch-grid phases and aggregating outputs in the original image coordinate system.

list.title: Do Coding Agents Deceive Us? Detecting and Preventing Cheating via Capped Evaluation with Randomized Tests

extra.desc: CapCode framework uses randomized testing with performance caps to detect and prevent shortcut exploitation in agent evaluation, while CapReward rewards systems that adhere to intended task specifications.

list.title: The Role of Feedback Alignment in Self-Distillation

extra.desc: Self-distillation effectiveness depends on structural alignment between feedback and solver reasoning, with step-aligned critique outperforming binary rewards and reference solutions by targeting specific reasoning failures.

list.title: Next Forcing: Causal World Modeling with Multi-Chunk Prediction

extra.desc: Next Forcing introduces a multi-chunk prediction framework that accelerates training and inference for autoregressive video generation while improving accuracy and physical law adherence.

list.title: FadeMem: Distance-Aware Memory Consolidation for Autoregressive Video Diffusion

extra.desc: FadeMem introduces a distance-aware key-value memory consolidation mechanism that organizes historical video data into a temporal hierarchy, improving long-video generation by preserving recent context and long-range anchors under fixed cache constraints.

list.title: Interpreting and Steering a Text-to-Speech Language Model with Sparse Autoencoders

extra.desc: Sparse autoencoders trained on language model representations reveal interpretable features for speech synthesis that can be manipulated to control linguistic and prosodic attributes.

list.title: Kwai Keye-VL-2.0 Technical Report

extra.desc: Kwai Keye-VL-2.0-30B-A3B is an open-source Mixture-of-Experts multimodal foundation model that enables long-video understanding and agentic intelligence through DeepSeek Sparse Attention and specialized training infrastructure.

list.title: IR3DE: A Linear Router for Large Language Models

extra.desc: A ridge regression-based routing method achieves competitive performance in selecting domain-expert LLMs for different tasks while enabling dynamic addition/removal of experts without retraining.

list.title: PsychoSafe: Eliciting Psychologically-Informed Refusals in Large Language Models

extra.desc: A psychologically-informed refusal framework called PsychoSafe is developed for large language models to improve harmful request handling through structured supportive communication, showing enhanced refusal quality and resource referral while maintaining performance on non-refusal tasks.

list.title: BrainSurgery: Reproducible and Reliable Declarative Weight Manipulations for Model Editing and Upcycling

extra.desc: BrainSurgery is a tool for robust and reproducible tensor manipulation of neural network checkpoints through declarative YAML plans with built-in validation.

list.title: UniPET: a universal network for high-quality PET image denoising across varied dose reduction factors

extra.desc: A universal PET image denoising framework addresses variability in dose reduction factors through domain generalization techniques and region-aware learning strategies.

list.title: U-TTT: Towards Generalizable PET Image Denoising via Test-Time Training

extra.desc: A novel U-shaped deep learning model with test-time training layers and dual-domain adaptation mechanisms achieves robust PET image denoising under distribution shifts.

list.title: Role-Agent: Bootstrapping LLM Agents via Dual-Role Evolution

extra.desc: Role-Agent framework enables LLM agents to function as both agent and environment through bootstrapped co-evolution, improving performance via environment-aware reasoning and targeted practice.

list.title: Late-Layer Fusion is Enough: Dual-Path Vision Token Routing for Multimodal Large Language Models under Visual Saturation

extra.desc: Research reveals that vision and text tokens in multimodal models evolve asynchronously, leading to inefficient computation; a new asymmetric routing framework reduces visual processing overhead while maintaining performance.

list.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism

extra.desc: MemDreamer addresses long-video understanding challenges by decoupling perception and reasoning through hierarchical graph memory and agentic exploration, achieving state-of-the-art performance with reduced computational overhead.

list.title: Retrospective Harness Optimization: Improving LLM Agents via Self-Preference over Trajectory Rollouts

extra.desc: Retrospective Harness Optimization (RHO) is a self-supervised method that improves AI agent performance by optimizing agent harness using only past trajectories through diverse task selection, parallel re-solving, and self-validation techniques.

list.title: Lip Forcing: Few-Step Autoregressive Diffusion for Real-time Lip Synchronization

extra.desc: Autoregressive diffusion method for video-to-video lip synchronization achieves real-time performance through distillation and optimized inference schedules.

list.title: Flow-DPPO: Divergence Proximal Policy Optimization for Flow Matching Models

extra.desc: Flow-DPPO replaces ratio clipping with divergence proximal constraints in flow matching models, improving training stability and multi-objective optimization through exact KL divergence computation.

list.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories

extra.desc: A multi-agent framework automates data journalism by generating evidence-grounded, multimodal news stories while maintaining transparency and verifiability.

list.title: WorldOlympiad: Can Your World Model Survive a Triathlon?

extra.desc: WorldOlympiad presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating video-based world models across physical faithfulness, geometric consistency, and interaction fidelity, revealing significant gaps in current generative models' capabilities.

list.title: Attention Amnesia in Hybrid LLMs: When CoT Fine-Tuning Breaks Long-Range Recall, and How to Fix It

extra.desc: Chain-of-thought supervised fine-tuning degrades long-context recall in hybrid linear-attention models by biasing attention gradients toward short-range patterns, but a training-free method called QK-Restore can restore long-context capabilities by reverting query-key projections while preserving reasoning performance.

list.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL

extra.desc: DRPO improves LLM reinforcement learning stability by replacing hard masks with smooth regularization that provides continuous gradient corrections beyond trust-region boundaries.

list.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents

extra.desc: EEVEE is a novel test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents that handles heterogeneous data streams through task clustering and co-evolving router-prompt optimization.

list.title: SearchSwarm: Towards Delegation Intelligence in Agentic LLMs for Long-Horizon Deep Research

extra.desc: A large language model trained on synthesized delegation intelligence achieves superior performance on long-horizon research tasks through task decomposition and subagent coordination.

list.title: One Token per Multimodal Evidence: Latent Memory for Resource-Constrained QA

extra.desc: Latent Memory introduces a compressed representation approach for external memory in question answering, reducing token consumption and storage requirements while maintaining competitive performance across text-only and multimodal benchmarks.

list.title: Struct-Searcher: Agentic Structural Thinking Advances Multimodal Deep Information Seeking

extra.desc: Struct-Searcher introduces a belief revision theory-based structural agentic workflow for multimodal information seeking that improves accuracy over existing vision-language models and deep research agents.

list.title: How Does Reasoning Flow? Tracing Attention-Induced Information Flow for Targeted RL in LLMs

extra.desc: FlowTracer is an RL framework that uses attention-induced graphs to trace reasoning flows and assign token-level credit based on global information propagation structures.

list.title: When the Chain of Thought Knows Better: Failure Modes in Multi-Turn Reasoning Models

extra.desc: Multi-turn reasoning models exhibit hidden alignment failures that are masked by traditional evaluation methods, revealing vulnerabilities through a trace-level diagnostic framework that identifies distinct failure modes including context-injection failures.

list.title: Emergent Misalignment Can Be Induced by Sycophancy and Reversed via Alignment Gating

extra.desc: Sycophancy fine-tuning contributes to emergent misalignment in language models, which can be reversed using Alignment Gating—a method that inserts learnable gates to identify and control unsafe responses while maintaining general capabilities.

list.title: ARM: An AutoRegressive Large Multimodal Model with Unified Discrete Representations

extra.desc: ARM demonstrates a unified autoregressive framework for image understanding, generation, and editing through discrete semantic tokenization and reinforcement learning optimization.

list.title: Bridging the Agent-World Gap: Text World Models for LLM-based Agents

extra.desc: Text world models serve as transition models for LLM-based agents in interactive environments, enabling planning and efficient learning by predicting environmental changes from textual states and actions.

list.title: Dynamic Linear Attention

extra.desc: DLA addresses limitations in long-context LLMs by introducing adaptive state merging and capacity-bounded memory modeling for improved multi-state linear attention.

list.title: SCAIL-2: Unifying Controlled Character Animation with End-to-end In-Context Conditioning

extra.desc: SCAIL-2 enables end-to-end character animation by directly transferring motion from driving videos without intermediate representations, using unified task decomposition and synthetic data generation.

list.title: Workflow-GYM: Towards Long-Horizon Evaluation of Computer-use Agentic tasks in Real-World Professional Fields

extra.desc: Current AI agents struggle with long-horizon professional GUI workflows, achieving low success rates due to issues with workflow consistency and domain-specific software understanding.

list.title: Test-Time Gradient Guidance of Flow Policies in Reinforcement Learning

extra.desc: QGF is an RL algorithm that improves policies at test time by using a value gradient to guide a pre-trained flow policy, avoiding training-time instability while maintaining competitive performance.

list.title: MilliVid: Hierarchical Latents for Long-Range Consistency in Video Generation

extra.desc: Video generative models achieve improved long-range consistency through coarse-to-fine token generation using a multi-scale autoencoder and diffusion model architecture.

list.title: ABot-Earth 0.5: Generative 3D Earth Model

extra.desc: ABot-Earth 0.5 generates realistic 3D environments from satellite imagery using 3D Gaussian Splatting representation, enabling fast synthesis and real-time visualization for Embodied AI applications.

list.title: Online Skill Learning for Web Agents via State-Grounded Dynamic Retrieval

extra.desc: State-Grounded Dynamic Retrieval enables web agents to dynamically reuse skills based on current webpage state rather than fixed task-level strategies, improving automation performance across multiple domains.

list.title: BenSyc: Benchmarking Conversational Sycophancy and Human Alignment in LLMs for Bengali Contexts

extra.desc: Researchers create BenSyc, a benchmark for evaluating conversational sycophancy in Bengali contexts, revealing challenges in distinguishing empathetic support from validation and escalation in emotionally sensitive dialogues.

list.title: What Should Agents Say? Action-state Communication for Efficient Multi-Agent Systems

extra.desc: Multi-agent systems using large language models suffer from inefficient token consumption in agent-to-agent communication, which PACT addresses by structuring messages as compact action-state records that improve performance-cost trade-offs across different system architectures.

list.title: VoLo: A Physical Orchestrator for Open-Vocabulary Long-Horizon Manipulation

extra.desc: VoLoAgent enables physical orchestration by integrating vision-language models with robot capabilities for open-vocabulary long-horizon manipulation tasks.

list.title: Trust Functions: Near-Lossless Weak-to-Strong Generalization by Learning When to Trust the Weak Teacher

extra.desc: Trust functions enable effective weak-to-strong generalization by identifying reliable weak labels for training, achieving performance comparable to ground-truth supervision across multiple domains.

list.title: Send a SCOUT First: Pre-hoc Reasoning for Adaptive Detector Allocation in Prompt-Injection Defense

extra.desc: SCOUT framework dynamically allocates prompt-injection detection by predicting detector reliability and latency, improving safety and efficiency over fixed single-detector approaches.

list.title: Precision Is Not Faithfulness: Coverage-Aware Evaluation of Grounded Generation with a Complete Oracle

extra.desc: Reference-free faithfulness metrics suffer from a blind spot measuring only precision, leading to rewards for abstention; completeness in deterministic domains enables measurement of both precision and recall, revealing that high-precision models often have poor fact coverage.

list.title: AsyncWebRL: Efficient Multi-Step RL for Visual Web Agents

extra.desc: AsyncWebRL improves vision-language web agent training through asynchronous reinforcement learning and trajectory normalization modifications, achieving faster throughput and better performance on challenging tasks.

list.title: SDR: Set-Distance Rewards for Radiology Report Generation

extra.desc: Set-based rewards using embedding distances improve chest X-ray report generation by enabling effective post-training and test-time selection without requiring causal reasoning structures.

list.title: Agents' Last Exam

extra.desc: Agents' Last Exam (ALE) is a benchmark for evaluating AI agents on long-term, economically valuable real-world tasks across 13 industry clusters with 1K+ tasks, revealing significant gaps between benchmark performance and practical deployment.

list.title: Robotic Policy Adaptation via Weight-Space Meta-Learning

extra.desc: WIZARD is a weight-space meta-learning framework that generates task-specific LoRA parameters for frozen VLA policies using language instructions and demonstration videos, enabling efficient task adaptation without fine-tuning.

list.title: Light-WAM: Efficient World Action Models with State-Fusion Action Decoding

extra.desc: Light-WAM is a lightweight world action model for robot manipulation that uses a compact video backbone and downsampled latent space for efficient future-video supervision, combined with a StateFusionActionExpert for direct action prediction.
